image.alt: Painted full-length portrait of a man in an ornate red-and-black military uniform with gold epaulettes and a star-studded sash, holding a document

rounds.revealFact: Toussaint Louverture led the only slave revolt in history to found a free state, setting the colony of Saint-Domingue on the road to becoming independent Haiti.

image.alt: Painting of a swirling cavalry battle among tents and banners, lit by fires and drifting smoke

attribution.fileTitle: File:Battle of Vienna (1683) by Józef Brandt.PNG
attribution.author: Józef Brandt

clues: A great imperial army besieged a walled capital for two months until a relief force swept down from the wooded hills above it.
A massive cavalry charge, remembered as one of the largest in history, broke the siege and marked the empire's furthest advance into the continent.

events.label: Copernicus argues that the Earth travels around the Sun
Francis Bacon sets out a method for modern experimental science
Robert Hooke first describes living cells under a microscope
Antonie van Leeuwenhoek observes tiny 'animalcules' through a lens

image.alt: Black-and-white photo looking down a long reflecting pool packed with an enormous crowd, a tall stone obelisk in the distance

attribution.author: Warren K. Leffler, U.S. News & World Report

followUpQuestion.prompt: At which memorial did the crowd gather to hear the day's speeches?

rounds.prompt: Japan's Meiji Restoration ends the rule of the shogun and begins a rush to modernize.
rounds.revealFact: The Meiji Restoration returned power to the emperor and launched Japan on a rapid transformation into an industrial world power.

rounds.prompt: Where stood the thousand-year capital of the Silla kingdom, dotted with grassy royal burial mounds and an ancient stone observatory?
rounds.revealFact: Gyeongju was the capital of Silla for nearly a thousand years and still preserves royal tombs and Cheomseongdae, one of the oldest surviving observatories in East Asia.
